resources.string: The app that doesn\'t read your messages and respects your privacy.
Required to receive SMS / MMS reliably on your phone.

Keeps SMS Tech alive in the background via a permanent notification in the status bar (mandatory Android contract for any foreground service — it cannot be dismissed while the toggle is ON). Useful on aggressive ROMs (Xiaomi MIUI/HyperOS, Huawei EMUI/HarmonyOS, Honor MagicOS, Oppo/Realme/OnePlus ColorOS, Vivo/iQOO FunTouch, Meizu Flyme, ASUS Zen UI) which otherwise kill third-party SMS apps and block MMS/notifications. Useless on Pixel, Samsung Galaxy, Sony, Fairphone, Motorola, Nokia, Nothing Phone — leave OFF on these.
Choose an action for this phone number.
No phone app available on this device.
No Contacts app available on this device.
No other conversation to forward to.
Recorded clip will be sent as an MMS to the recipient.
Every number in this conversation will be blocked. You can unblock them later in Settings.
Permanently delete every conversation whose participants are all on the system blocklist.
Biometric not available on this device — set a PIN first
Search a number, a contact, a message…
A modern, private SMS & MMS app. No ads, no trackers, no analytics. Your conversations stay on your device.
We need SMS, MMS, contacts and notification permissions to work. We explain every single one.
Android only lets a single app send and receive SMS. Once selected, you can change your mind anytime in system settings.
Pick your language and look. You can change them later in Settings.
Once you receive or send a message, it will appear here.
Reading your existing SMS — this happens only once.
All messages in this conversation will be permanently removed from this device. Other conversations are not affected.
Delete this message? This cannot be undone.
Microphone access is needed to record voice messages.
Microphone permission denied. Open Settings to enable it.
Search a contact or phone number
Unlock the app to access the vault.

Conversations you place here are hidden from the main list. They remain stored on your device (encrypted with SQLCipher). Note: SMS is unencrypted on the carrier network. For end-to-end encryption use Signal or Matrix.
Conversation moved out of the vault
Choose how the emergency call buttons act when tapped.
Tap opens the phone app with the number pre-filled. You tap the green button to actually call. No special permission. Default.
Hold the button 3 seconds → immediate call to 112 or 17 without confirmation. Requires the CALL_PHONE permission. Anti-pocket-dial via mandatory hold-3s.
Call permission denied. Falling back to dialer.
To enable hold-3s direct call, SMS Tech needs the CALL_PHONE permission. If you refuse, the buttons will keep their default behavior (open dialer).
False alert. I am OK. Please do not worry, no action needed.
This will reset the emergency cooldown and (optionally) send a short SMS to your safety-call contacts so they know it was a false alert. You can disable the SMS in Settings.
Cooldown reset, but no contact received the SMS. Try again or contact them manually.
Send "I am OK" SMS on reset
When you tap "I am OK" after a false-alarm trigger, also send a short SMS to your safety-call contacts saying it was a false alert.
Adds a Google Maps link with your current location to the emergency SMS body. Requires location permission. If refused, the SMS is sent without coordinates.
Location permission denied. GPS inclusion disabled.
The big URGENCE button and the lock-screen shortcut will stop working until you reactivate them in Settings. You can always come back to this page later.
To call 112 / 15 / 17 / 18 directly without going through the dialer, SMS Tech needs the CALL_PHONE permission. If you refuse, taps will fall back to the dialer (you confirm).
Which relative do you want to call?
Enter the PIN or password that protects the vault. You can also use biometrics if available.
Distinct PIN or password for the vault
When ON, opening the vault asks for a dedicated PIN/password (and optionally biometrics) even if the app is already unlocked. The credential is hashed separately from the app PIN.
Pick a PIN that is different from your app PIN.
PINs do not match. Try again.
PIN must be at least 4 characters.
Choose a PIN that is different from your app PIN.
Confirm your identity to open the private folder.
Flags suspicious incoming SMS (shortened URLs, urgency wording, premium-rate numbers, lookalike domains). 100% local, no cloud. May produce false positives.
SMS Tech detected the following suspicious signals in this message. No SMS content was sent anywhere — everything is computed locally.
Shortened URL (bit.ly, t.co, tinyurl…). Shorteners can hide the real destination — verify before tapping.
Urgency or pressure wording (urgent, expires, click here…). A common social-engineering tactic to trigger panic.
Premium-rate phone number (3xxx, 0899xxx, 081x…). Calling these numbers is expensive and often part of a scam.
Look-alike domain (e.g. "1mpots.gouv.fr" instead of "impots.gouv.fr"). Always type official addresses by hand.
Unlock the app to move this conversation
Silent persistent notification with quick-dial actions (112/17). Tap the body to open the Emergency screen, then hold 3 s to send the alert.
Tap to open the emergency page, or dial 112.
Show a persistent notification with quick-dial actions (112/17) accessible without unlocking. Tap the body to open the Emergency screen. Off by default.
Police 17 quick action in lock-screen notif
Add a "Dial 17" quick action to the persistent emergency lock-screen notification. The 17 button is always visible in the in-app Emergency page regardless of this setting. Off by default.
Encrypted backup uses AES-256-GCM with PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 key derivation. Pick a strong passphrase — without it the file cannot be recovered.
Choose a passphrase to encrypt the backup. At least 8 characters. Keep it safe — there is no recovery.
Restore from encrypted file lands in v1.1 — current export already produces a fully self-contained file.
Reads from the system SMS provider and mirrors messages into the encrypted database. SMS Tech must already be the default SMS app.
✨ Thanks to those who care about their privacy. ✨
Too many attempts. Try again in %1$d s.
Developer-friendly dark palette. Overrides dynamic colors and AMOLED.
Optional. If set, this number replaces the AOSP placeholder \"insert-address-token\" in the sender address of outgoing MMS, so other SMS apps display you correctly.
Cannot read the SIM number — enter it manually
Phone-numbers permission not granted — enter it manually
Must be a valid phone number (+ and digits)
Deletes SMS, MMS and voice messages older than the chosen age. Automatic cleanup runs at most once a month; you can also trigger a manual cleanup at any time. Starred messages are never deleted.
Auto runs once a month. Starred messages are never deleted.
No messages to delete for this duration.
%1$d message(s) older than %2$d days will be deleted. Starred messages are kept. This cannot be undone.
Re-imports all SMS from the Android system. Useful to recover history deleted by mistake. No duplicates.

When you post an emoji reaction (heart, thumbs-up…), a SMS containing only the emoji is sent to the recipient. Counts as a normal SMS at your plan\'s rate. Removing or changing a reaction does not resend anything.
Choose how reactions are sent over SMS to recipients who don\'t use SMS Tech.
"J\'ai réagi par ❤️ à : «preview»". Natural and understandable for any French-speaking Android recipient. Decoded by SMS Tech as a native reaction badge.
"Reacted ❤️ to «preview»". Detected automatically by iMessage (iPhone) and recent Google Messages as a native reaction bubble. Less natural for French recipients.
"❤️" alone. Cleaner on older SMS apps, but the recipient loses all context — they can\'t tell which message you reacted to.
"❤️ «your message»". Compact and context-preserving. Recipient sees emoji followed by the quoted original message.
Included in outgoing reaction SMS to help the recipient identify the author. Leave empty to use your Android profile name (or fall back to anonymous "Reacted ❤️…").
Send an automatic SMS to your emergency contacts if you don\'t open SMS Tech for a configured duration. Designed for solo travel, isolated living, professions at risk.
When enabled, opening SMS Tech resets the timer. If you don\'t open it within the chosen duration, your contacts receive an automatic SMS.
In hours · 168 h = 1 week · max 720 h (30 days)
Add at least one contact before enabling.
Safety call will trigger in about %1$d hours. Tap to reset the timer.
Safety call will trigger in less than 2 hours. Tap to reset now.
A hold-3s button sends an SMS to your emergency contacts with your current location. Distinct from Safety call (which is automatic on inactivity). Disabled by default.
Emergency mode is disabled. Turn it on in settings.
No emergency contacts configured. Add some in Safety call settings.
Just sent. Available again in a minute.
Hold the button 3 seconds to alert your contacts.
Emergency sent to %1$d contact(s) — location unavailable.
Emergency send failed (%1$d). Check your default SMS app.
When on, an emergency button appears in the dedicated screen. Hold 3s to send an SMS with your location to your emergency contacts.
Adds a Google Maps URL to the SMS. Requires Location permission. If turned off (or permission denied), the SMS is sent without coordinates.
Location permission not granted. The emergency SMS will be sent without coordinates. Grant the permission in Android settings to fix this.
An SMS containing only this emoji will be sent to your recipient. Counts as a regular SMS (plan rate). You can disable this in Settings → Sending.
All current unread badges will be cleared. New incoming messages will display badges normally afterwards.
Re-imports messages from the system SMS/MMS provider. Existing messages stay (deduped via unique ID); previously deleted messages may reappear if they still exist in the system database. Continue?
All your preferences (theme, notifications, sending, etc.) will return to default. Your conversations and messages are NOT affected.
Deletes every conversation whose participants are entirely in the blocklist. This also removes them from the system SMS provider. Irreversible.
Controls what shows up on the lock screen and in heads-up notifications.
Sender name and a redacted body show on the lock screen; the full content appears once unlocked.
Plays the sound but nothing appears on the lock screen — fully invisible until you unlock and open the app.
Banner pops over the current screen with sound, then docks in the shade.
No pop-up, no sound. Just an icon in the shade.
Routed to a low-importance channel. The system masks sound and the heads-up pop-up.
Check or fix permissions, channels, and sound at the OS level.
Notifications are disabled for SMS Tech in the system settings. Tap below to fix.
Hides the conversation thumbnail in the system task switcher and blocks screenshots / screen recording while the app is open.
Automatically locks the encrypted vault as soon as the app goes to the background. You will need to unlock again on return.
SMS Tech is the default SMS app.
All conversations and settings will be wiped. This cannot be undone.
Modern, private SMS & MMS for Android.

SMS and MMS travel unencrypted across the carrier network — this is a protocol limitation, not a choice. SMS Tech only protects what is stored on your device. For end-to-end encrypted messaging, use Signal or Matrix.
Created by %1$s. UI inspired by Material 3 motion principles. Built with Kotlin, Compose, Hilt, Room and SQLCipher.
Notifications for SMS and MMS you receive.
Notifications when SMS Tech is set to "Silent" mode. No heads-up, no sound.
Reminder before automatic SMS to your emergency contacts.
SMS Tech is not the default SMS app yet.
MMS limit reached (280 KB). Remove an attachment or shorten the text.
Default SMS app for Android 8 → 15+. Full history import, no duplicates.
Push-to-talk over MMS. Capped at 60 s / 300 KB for compatibility with all French carriers.
Sensitive conversations in a SQLCipher vault, key wrapped in the Android Keystore.
A second PIN opens the app in decoy mode — vault invisible and inaccessible.
Hold the button 3 s on the Emergency page → alert SMS prefixed ⚠️ to your Safety Call contacts with optional GPS. Quick access 112 / 15 / 17 / 18 + lockscreen notification shortcuts.
Fingerprint or face to unlock, with a mandatory fallback PIN.
Mini-card above the composer + reply bubble with quote in the thread.
Long-press → \"Translate\" delegates to your installed translation app (Google Translate, DeepL, Aves Translate FLOSS …) via system Intent. No model bundled.
Auto-import of Phone / Samsung Messages blocked numbers. Retroactive purge.
SQLite FTS4 full-text search over bodies, numbers, and contact names.
Export a conversation to PDF (date, sender, body) for archive or proof.
Local AES-256-GCM backup with a passphrase distinct from the PIN.
Light, dark, AMOLED, Dark Tech (slate-blue + blue accent). Customizable rounded corners.
Compose + per-bubble isolated recomposition. Conversation sorting in the 3-dot menu.
No analytics. Only network connection: the carrier MMSC for MMS transport.
Blue button \"Set as default\" at the top of the conversation list
Or Android Settings → Default apps → SMS → SMS Tech
Long-press the mic to the right of the composer
Speak, then release to send (slide left = cancel)
Cap 60 s — recording stops automatically beyond that
Move a conversation to the vault
Long-press the conversation in the list → \"Vault\"
Unlock: PIN, passphrase or biometrics depending on your setting
Settings → Security to configure the vault
In a conversation: menu ⋮ → Block (= blocks + deletes the conv)
Long-press a conversation in the list → Block (keeps history)
The number is added to the system blocklist, shared with the Phone app
A picker opens with all installed translation apps
Choose Google Translate, DeepL, Aves Translate (FLOSS) or another
If none is installed, a message invites you to install one
Settings → Emergency mode: enable + add at least one Safety Call contact
Emergency page: direct calls 112 / 15 / 17 / 18; red button held 3 s → SMS to relatives
\"I am OK\" in the conversation list cancels the alert in case of accidental trigger
\"View updates\" button at the top of this page
Opens the SMS Tech GitHub Releases page
The app never attempts to auto-update without your consent
Message from %1$s too large (%2$d KB). Tap to open MMS settings.
Failed to download MMS from %1$s. Check signal coverage and retry.
The previous attempt may have already reached the recipient. Resending could create a duplicate.

plurals.name: vault_bulk_move_out_done
plurals.item: %d conversation moved out of the vault
%d conversations moved out of the vault
